---
title: "Ajinkya Ranade, PhD"
image: Ajinkya.jpg
toc: false
about:
  template: trestles
  image-shape: rounded
  image-width: 23em
  links:
    - icon: linkedin
      text: LinkedIn
      href: https://www.linkedin.com/in/ajinkya-ranade-phd-b4b899130/
      target: _blank
    - icon: "{{< ai orcid >}}"
      text: "{{< ai orcid >}} ORCID"
      href: https://orcid.org/my-orcid?orcid=0000-0003-3945-2656
      target: _blank
    - icon: "{{< ai researchgate >}}" 
      text: "{{< ai researchgate >}} Researchgate"
      href: https://www.researchgate.net/profile/Ajinkya-Ranade
      target: _blank
    - icon: "{{< ai google-scholar >}}"
      text: "{{< ai google-scholar >}} Google Scholar"
      href: https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=Yir-Mq8AAAAJ&hl=en
      target: _blank
    - icon: envelope
      text: Email
      href: mailto:ajinkyaranade3@gmail.com
      target: _blank
    - icon: file-earmark-pdf
      text: CV
      href: Ajinkya_Ranade_CV.pdf
      target: _blank
---

<!--
::: {style="text-align: justify"}


I am Ajinkya Ranade, currently working as JSPS postdoctoral fellow at [Institute of Science Tokyo](https://www.titech.ac.jp/english){target="_blank"}, Japan. My research focus is on innovating efficient, sustainable and [**fit-for-purpose**]{.underline} water treatment technologies [**for water recycling and energy conservation.**]{.underline}

An analytical chemist with interdisciplinary research experience in graphene technology, semiconductor technology, optoelectronics, and wastewater treatment exploring opportunities to innovate **sustainable water treatment technologies** in the research and development sector. I aim to utilize my interdisciplinary knowledge to tackle **water-energy nexus and provide energy efficient and recyclable water treatment solutions** particularly for developing nations. Currently looking for organizations that provides me ample scope to nurture this interest and develop my skills.

-   Expertise in individual water treatment technologies (adsorption, photocatalysis, biodegradation ) and synergic water treatment technology (intimately coupled photocatalytic biodegradation) with a critical focus on sustainability and energy efficiency

-   Expertise in materials engineering, graphene technology, semiconductor technology and optoelectronics

-   Expertise in fabrication of high-quality monolayer graphene using (atmospheric and low pressure)\
    chemical vapor deposition techniques

-   Published 3 first author publication and 9 co-author publications

-   Recent publication in the prestigious **Water Research** Journal
:::
-->

::: {style="text-align: justify"}

I am an R&D scientist working at the intersection of advanced water treatment, semiconductor materials science, graphene technology and sustainable process engineering. My work is driven by a clear ambition: to develop foundational technologies that redefine industrial wastewater treatment, making systems more efficient,energy positive, scalable, and environmentally sustainable.

I aim to contribute at a level where core technological breakthroughs translate into widespread industrial impact, shaping the future of water infrastructure. This vision draws inspiration from how Prof. Shuji Nakamura’s (Nobel Prize in Physics 2014) work on blue LEDs at Nichia transformed modern electronics by establishing a new technological foundation rather than incremental improvement. 

**Impact Highlights**

1) **Patent filed within 7 months of industry transition** — immediate translation of research into commercially protectable innovation

2) **First-author publication in Water Research (2025)** — peer-reviewed evidence of breakthrough results in pharmaceutical pollutant remediation

3) **13x faster kinetic degradation of pharmaceutical antibiotics compared to conventional photocatalysis**

4) **90% reduction in electrical energy per order versus conventional photocatalysis**

5) **JSPS Postdoctoral Fellow** — internationally competitive research fellowship, Tokyo Institute of Technology

6) **Dual India-Japan research and industry background** — uniquely positioned to bridge both markets

**Strategic Interests**

1) Advanced biological-photocatalytic water treatment systems for industrial pharmaceutical and organic pollutant remediation

2) Translating laboratory-proven treatment innovations into scalable, commercially deployable solutions

3) Japan-India technology bridge: leveraging bilateral government frameworks to expand advanced water treatment into high-growth Asian markets

4) Semiconductor-integrated photocatalytic systems for next-generation treatment efficiency

5) Converting R&D breakthroughs into protectable intellectual property with commercial applications
:::
